source_pr.title: test: improve test by checking gc_delete_range instead of scanning keys
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #27888

Problem Summary: 

Previously in the tests about `drop table` and `drop index`, we asserted an table/index is dropped by scanning the key in new txn in a period of time(15s) until it is not found. This brings uncertainty so that it failed when the CI machine is in a high load.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Tests asserted `DROP TABLE/INDEX` completion by scanning KV keys in a loop with a fixed time window (e.g. ~15s); under high CI load this could exceed the window and fail intermittently.
failure_signature: Tests asserted `DROP TABLE/INDEX` completion by scanning KV keys in a loop with a fixed time window (e.g. ~15s); under high CI load this could exceed the window and fail intermittently.
root_cause_explanation: The tests were flaky because they used a time-bounded loop that repeatedly scanned KV keys to decide whether a `DROP TABLE` / `DROP INDEX` cleanup finished. This is load-sensitive and can time out intermittently on busy CI machines. This PR stabilizes the tests by checking the GC delete-range bookkeeping tables (e.g. `mysql.gc_delete_range_done`) instead of scanning keys with a fixed timeout.
fix_pattern: Replace time-bounded key-scanning loops with deterministic checks against GC delete-range bookkeeping (`mysql.gc_delete_range_done`) to avoid load-sensitive timeouts.

evidence: evidence: // It appears in the main function to be set before any use of client-go to prevent data race.
